The provided multi-node StatefulSet readinessProbe uses `cqlsh -e ...` without credentials; with PasswordAuthenticator enabled that probe would fail, so I added `-u cassandra -p cassandra` to the probe in my own manifest — a template caveat for auth-enabled rings, not a repo edit.

topology: ring (2 NORMAL StatefulSet nodes + 1 joiner bare pod), single DC, cassandra:4.0.1; A/B control identical topology on cassandra:4.0.2
trigger: A node in BOOT mode (held via -Dcassandra.ring_delay_ms=600000, observed as UJ in nodetool status). While it is in BOOT, CREATE KEYSPACE ks15433 (SimpleStrategy RF=2) + CREATE TABLE + 50 INSERTs are issued through a NORMAL coordinator (cass-0). The keyspace did not exist when the BOOT state change was observed, so pending ranges are not recalculated on its creation, and the joining node is excluded from the writes.
verbatim_signature: Local write count: 0
evidence: On buggy 4.0.1, after 50 RF=2 writes were acked through NORMAL coordinator cass-0 while the joiner was UJ (Mode: JOINING / BOOT), `nodetool cfstats ks15433.t` on the joiner reported `Write Count: 0` and `Local write count: 0` for the keyspace it KNEW about (schema gossiped: Keyspace ks15433 / Table t appear in cfstats), i.e. zero of the 50 writes reached the pending replica. The IDENTICAL workload on fixed 4.0.2 — same UJ/BOOT state, same RF=2 keyspace created during BOOT, same 50 INSERTs via the NORMAL coordinator — produced `Write Count: 36` / `Local write count: 36` on the joiner (writes routed to the pending ranges it owns). The 0-vs-36 contrast under identical bootstrap state is the proof; 0 is the bug, not merely "still bootstrapping". Both the literal `Local write count: 0` (buggy) and `Local write count: 36` (fixed) full cfstats blocks are in /tmp/repro-CASSANDRA-15433.md.
control: A/B with fixed image cassandra:4.0.2 (fixVersions list 4.0.2; 4.0.2 <= 4.0.20 ceiling). Identical namespace/topology/workload rebuilt on 4.0.2: 2 NORMAL nodes + joiner bare pod with ring_delay_ms=600000; joiner reached UJ/BOOT and logged "JOINING: sleeping 600000 ms for pending range setup"; same CREATE KEYSPACE ks15433 RF=2 + 50 RF=2 INSERTs via NORMAL coordinator cass-0 while joiner UJ. Result: joiner cfstats showed Local write count: 36 (vs 0 on 4.0.1). The fix recalculates pending ranges on keyspace creation, so the joining node receives writes — confirming the buggy behavior is a regression fixed in 4.0.2.
notes: Mechanism per Jira body (Cluster/Membership): pending tokens are recalculated for keyspaces that EXIST when the BOOT state change is observed (StorageService.handleState*); keyspaces CREATED while a node is in BOOT/BOOT_REPLACE do NOT trigger a pending-range recalc (around Schema.merge), so the joining node receives zero writes for them -> silent data loss after bootstrap. Followed the body's exact reproducer steps 1-4; step 4 (nodetool cfstats records zero writes on the joining node) is the author-blessed symptom and is what I captured. Did NOT wait out the 600s ring_delay to show post-bootstrap loss — that is the downstream consequence; the enumerated reproducer is the cfstats observation, captured within budget. The "sleeping ... for pending range setup" log line moved from StorageService:1619 (4.0.1) to :1633 (4.0.2); kept only as a harmless side note, NOT as fix-evidence (the 0-vs-36 contrast is the real evidence). Joiner CQL transport is down during BOOT (cqlsh ConnectionRefused on 9042) so observation was via nodetool/JMX, and the joiner was deployed as a bare pod (not in the StatefulSet) so the cqlsh readiness probe would not block forever. Used minimal data (50 small rows); no stress, no PVC, ephemeral storage.
tag_correction: none — the classifier hint (topology=ring, confidence=H, trigger: node in BOOT + CREATE KEYSPACE + writes -> joining node receives zero writes / data loss after bootstrap) matches the Jira body exactly.
tooling_findings: `kind load docker-image cassandra:4.0.1` and `cassandra:4.0.2` both FAILED with `ctr: content digest sha256:<...> not found` (and `kind load image-archive` on a docker-save tar failed the same way with a different missing digest). Root cause: kind's importer runs `docker exec <node> ctr --namespace=k8s.io images import --all-platforms --digests --snapshotter=overlayfs -`, and `--all-platforms --digests` demands multi-platform manifest blobs that are absent from the single-platform images / docker-save tars available on this host. Workaround used (record-only, not a fix): `docker save cassandra:<v> -o t.tar`, then on each of the 4 kind nodes `docker exec -i <node> ctr --namespace=k8s.io images import --snapshotter=overlayfs - < t.tar` (dropping --all-platforms/--digests). This correctly tagged docker.io/library/cassandra:4.0.1 and :4.0.2 on all nodes and pods ran fine with imagePullPolicy: IfNotPresent. SREGym's automated image-load path may hit this same kind/ctr incompatibility on hosts whose local images lack multi-platform manifests.

topology: Ring, multi-DC. Two single-node Cassandra pods in kind ns repro-16334, one per datacenter (dc1=cass-dc1 10.244.1.130, dc2=cass-dc2 10.244.1.131), GossipingPropertyFileSnitch, cluster "repro", max_mutation_size_in_kb:1000 appended to cassandra.yaml on each, ephemeral storage. nodetool status showed 2 datacenters both UN. Keyspace repro16334_ks = NetworkTopologyStrategy {dc1:1, dc2:1}. RF=1/DC was sufficient (mechanism is RF-independent); the report's 6-node 3:3 RF=3 ring was NOT needed.
trigger: On the buggy 4.0.1 2-DC ring, with consistency LOCAL_ONE (mandatory — set via `CONSISTENCY LOCAL_ONE;`; cqlsh's default ONE would mask the bug), INSERT a single ~1.1MB-byte blob (0x + 'ab'*1100000, 2.2MB hex) into a NetworkTopologyStrategy{dc1:1,dc2:1} table. The blob exceeds max_mutation_size_in_kb=1000 so every replica rejects it. Insert run from a CQL file (cqlsh -f) to avoid ARG_MAX. Buggy result: WriteTimeout code=1100 instead of the correct WriteFailure code=1500.
verbatim_signature: WriteTimeout: Error from server: code=1100 [Coordinator node timed out waiting for replica nodes' responses] message="Operation timed out - received only 0 responses." info={'consistency': 'LOCAL_ONE', 'required_responses': 1, 'received_responses': 0, 'write_type': 'SIMPLE'}
evidence: Three runs, identical workload (LOCAL_ONE + 2.2MB-hex blob insert into table t), only topology varied. (1) BUGGY 4.0.1, multi-DC keyspace {dc1:1,dc2:1}: WriteTimeout code=1100 [Coordinator node timed out waiting for replica nodes' responses] ... 'consistency':'LOCAL_ONE','required_responses':1,'received_responses':0,'write_type':'SIMPLE' (THE BUG, matches Jira). (2) BUGGY 4.0.1, single-DC keyspace {dc1:1} (report's own contrast): WriteFailure code=1500 [Replica(s) failed to execute write] message="Operation failed - received 0 responses and 1 failures: UNKNOWN from /10.244.1.130:7000" ... 'failures':1 (CORRECT). (3) FIXED 4.0.2, identical multi-DC keyspace {dc1:1,dc2:1}: WriteFailure code=1500 [Replica(s) failed to execute write] message="Operation failed - received 0 responses and 1 failures: UNKNOWN from /10.244.2.108:7000" ... 'failures':1 (CORRECT — fix confirmed). The only variable flipping correct<->buggy is multi-DC on unfixed code, exactly as CASSANDRA-16334 states.
control: A/B control with FIXED image cassandra:4.0.2 (buggy patch+1, <= 4.0 ceiling of 20; 4.0.2 is in Jira fixVersions). Deployed a second 2-DC ring in the same namespace (fix-dc1 dc1 10.244.2.108, fix-dc2 dc2 10.244.3.107, cluster "reprofix", both UN). Ran the IDENTICAL multi-DC keyspace {dc1:1,dc2:1} + identical LOCAL_ONE oversized-blob insert. Result: WriteFailure code=1500 (correct) instead of the buggy WriteTimeout code=1100 — clean A/B. Secondary control: same buggy 4.0.1 image on a single-DC keyspace gave the correct WriteFailure code=1500 (the report's own contrast). Both controls converge.
notes: Bug mechanism: with a DC-local consistency level (LOCAL_ONE, the DataStax-driver default the report used), the coordinator's write-response handler (DatacenterWriteResponseHandler) counts total replica endpoints across ALL DCs but only tallies FAILURE responses from the local DC. When every replica rejects the oversized mutation, the failure threshold is never reached, so the coordinator waits out the full write timeout -> WriteTimeout (code=1100) instead of WriteFailure (code=1500). Two critical reproduction requirements confirmed empirically: (a) consistency must be LOCAL_ONE — cqlsh's default ONE routes through the all-failures-counting handler and yields the correct WriteFailure, masking the bug; (b) keyspace must use NetworkTopologyStrategy spanning 2+ DCs. RF=1 per DC was enough — no 6-node ring needed, keeping the host within disk/memory budget. Both images were pre-cached on kind nodes (no pull). Reproduced in ~3 deploy cycles within budget.
tag_correction: Hint trigger said "2-DC (RF=3 each)"; reality: the bug is RF-independent and reproduces with the minimal RF=1 per DC (2-node ring total, not the report's 6-node 3:3 RF=3). topology=ring (multi-DC) and confidence=H were CORRECT. The hint omitted the load-bearing detail that consistency must be LOCAL_ONE (driver default) — with cqlsh's default ONE the bug does not manifest.
tooling_findings: None. Both cassandra:4.0.1 (buggy) and cassandra:4.0.2 (fixed control) were already cached on the kind worker nodes, so no image build/pull was needed. The benign trailing-semicolon SyntaxException emitted by cqlsh during the heredoc CREATE/DESCRIBE step did not affect schema creation (keyspace and table were created successfully and used by the inserts).

topology: ring (2-node StatefulSet in kind, RF=2, ephemeral storage), namespace repro-16307, keyspace ks16307, table t(pk int, ck int, PRIMARY KEY (pk,ck))
trigger: 2-node RF=2 ring; INSERT (0,0) and (1,1) at CL=ALL; create per-replica mirror divergence by node-local deletes (the dtest's executeInternal emulated via gossip isolation): delete (0,0) on cass-0 only and (1,1) on cass-1 only, each issued on the node CONFIRMED to see its peer DOWN, with hinted handoff DISABLED so divergence is not healed by hint replay. After re-forming the ring (2 UN), run CONSISTENCY ALL; PAGING 1; SELECT * FROM ks16307.t GROUP BY pk; FIRST run before blocking read-repair heals the divergence.
verbatim_signature:
Consistency level set to ALL.
Page size: 1

 pk | ck
----+----
  0 |  0

(1 rows)

Warnings :
Aggregation query used without partition key
evidence: On cassandra:3.11.10 the paged GROUP BY at CL=ALL wrongly returns deleted row (0,0) as "(1 rows)". sstabledump on each node proved mirror divergence BEFORE the query: cass-0 had pk=1 alive (tstamp 07:08:03) and pk=0 deleted (marked_deleted 07:11:10); cass-1 had pk=0 alive (07:08:03) and pk=1 deleted (07:09:56). Both delete timestamps > insert timestamps, so CL=ALL reconciliation must yield 0 rows. Within-version contrast on the SAME diverged data (3.11.10): PAGING OFF GROUP BY -> 0 rows (correct); plain SELECT no GROUP BY -> 0 rows (correct); only PAGING 1 + GROUP BY -> 1 spurious row. A/B fixed-image control on cassandra:3.11.11 (the exact fixVersion, release_version verified): identical mirror divergence reproduced (cass-0 pk=1 alive/pk=0 deleted; cass-1 pk=0 alive/pk=1 deleted via sstabledump) and the SAME query "CONSISTENCY ALL; PAGING 1; SELECT * FROM ks16307.t GROUP BY pk;" returned "(0 rows)" (correct). Buggy=1 spurious deleted row, Fixed=0 rows.
control: A/B fixed-image control performed. Redeployed the SAME 2-node ring in namespace repro-16307 with cassandra:3.11.11 (the listed fixVersion; 3.11.10+1=11 <= 3.11 ceiling 19; release_version 3.11.11 confirmed). Ran the identical workload (INSERT at CL=ALL, same gossip-isolation mirror divergence verified via sstabledump: cass-0 pk=1 alive/pk=0 deleted, cass-1 pk=0 alive/pk=1 deleted) and the identical query CONSISTENCY ALL; PAGING 1; SELECT * FROM ks16307.t GROUP BY pk; -> returned (0 rows), CORRECT. Plus within-version contrast on 3.11.10 itself: non-paged and non-GROUP-BY variants on the same diverged data both correctly returned 0 rows, isolating the defect to the paged GROUP BY coordination path.
notes: The Jira reproducer is an in-JVM dtest using executeInternal for per-node local deletes. This was faithfully reproduced on a real kind ring. Two critical pitfalls: (1) hinted handoff MUST be disabled before the isolated deletes, else the tombstone replays on gossip reconnect and heals divergence; (2) the first CL=ALL query triggers blocking read repair and heals the divergence, so the buggy signature must be captured on the FIRST run and the fixed control needs its own fresh divergence (done via full StatefulSet redeploy). Also note nodetool disablegossip is asymmetric: the node that disables its own gossip freezes its view (keeps peer UP and would still forward CL=ONE mutations to it); the peer that keeps gossip ON correctly marks the silent node DOWN. So each local delete had to be issued on the node confirmed to see its peer DOWN, in two opposite isolation phases. dclocal_read_repair_chance=0.1 (10% background read repair) was a minor risk; kept reads minimal during the isolated phase. Used only 2 rows, ephemeral storage, no stress, ~13.8GiB free reported throughout.

Two environment notes for whoever automates this bug: (1) sstabledump is not on PATH in the cassandra:3.11.x images; full path is /opt/cassandra/tools/bin/sstabledump. (2) The multi-node ring template's readinessProbe (initialDelaySeconds 90) makes each pod take ~2-3 min to become Ready; a 2-node OrderedReady rollout is ~4-6 min, so deploy+control is a real time sink. (3) A multi-statement cqlsh -e string with a leading newline before CREATE KEYSPACE produced a spurious "SyntaxException: no viable alternative at input ';'" but the substantive statements still executed; harmless but noted.

trigger: 2-node ring on cassandra:3.11.10; decommission one node (cassB) and delete its pod so it lingers in gossip with STATUS:LEFT + stale SCHEMA; CREATE KEYSPACE k on the surviving seed (advances its schema while the removed node's gossip entry keeps the OLD version); launch a fresh-identity bootstrapper (cassC). cassC aborts startup waiting for schema agreement from the removed node. NOTE: on this Docker Hub 3.11.10 build the abort fires at the GENERAL join-time schema wait (joinTokenRing:987 -> waitForSchema:947) and does NOT require allocate_tokens_for_keyspace (proven by a discriminating test: cassc crashes identically with allocate_tokens_for_keyspace=null). The Jira reporter hit the same broken waitForSchema via the allocate_tokens_for_keyspace path (allocateTokens:206).
